title: galarhoeus subcordatus
intro: Galarhoeus subcordatus, also known as the Chinese milk vetch or hairy milk vetch, is a perennial plant native to China that belongs to the legume family. Traditionally, it has been used in Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its bioactive compounds, which have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory, anti-bacterial, and anti-viral effects, leading to several health benefits such as improved immune function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced wound healing. In herbal preparations, Galarhoeus subcordatus is often combined with other herbs and used as a decoction or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
